title: Ringer's Solution
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"eve1m","text":"Standard protocol for preparing 100mL of Ringer's Solution for mice and rat perfusions. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
guidelines: {"blocks":[{"key":"c0upe","text":"Make sure you bubble for at least 5 minutes before adding calcium chloride to Ringer's solution to prevent precipitation. If precipitation occurs, discard and remake entire solution. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: 1.0 mL (Na 2 HPO 4 , 18 g/L, at room temperature)
1.0 ml heparin (prevents blood clotting) —bubble 95% air: 5% CO 2 at 35 ºC
Bring solution up to 97ml with double distilled water (ddH 2 O).
creator: National Center for Microscopy and Imaging Research
